prompt: Pull all senior data engineer postings on Greenhouse this week and rank them by match score.
Show jobs from companies in fintech, NYC or remote, that explicitly list TypeScript.
Adapt the same resume bullets I used for Vercel into something for Cloudflare's senior platform role.
This job description emphasizes async / distributed work. Reorder my resume to lead with my remote-first experience.
Identify which 5 jobs from this week are stretch roles vs solid matches based on my background.
Track which application platforms have the highest reply rate over the last 30 days.
How do you decide what counts as a strong match vs a stretch?
Can you submit applications overnight while I sleep?
How do you avoid sending the same resume twice?
What does 'flag for manual steps' mean in your reports?
I think you're scoring too many of these as Tier 1. Tighten the criteria — only true high-match.
Skip the overnight batch tonight, I want to review each Tier 1 manually first.
When a form asks why I'm leaving my current job, what's a safe truthful answer that doesn't badmouth the employer?
Run the overnight batch of 54 applications — full Tier 1 customization on all of them.
